scenes.summary: Dante introduces the impossibility of fully describing the horrific wounds he has witnessed.
Dante compares the current carnage to the historical bloodshed in Puglia and other Italian battles.
Dante describes the gruesome sight of Muhammad, whose body is split open from chin to groin.
Muhammad explains how he and his followers are eternally being cut by a demon for sowing discord.
Virgil clarifies that he brought Dante into Hell to provide a complete experience of the suffering.
Muhammad delivers a specific message regarding the fate of Fra Dolcino's followers.
A soul with a pierced throat and no nose identifies himself and addresses Dante personally.
The mutilated soul delivers an oracle concerning the fate of certain Italian lords and a betrayal at sea.
Dante asks for clarification, and a companion soul identifies the mutilated figure as Curio.

scenes.scene_name: The Encounter with the Severed Hands
scenes.summary: A soul with both hands missing appears and quotes a bitter remark about the destruction of a lineage.
Dante witnesses a headless bust that carries its own severed head by the hair to use as a light.
The headless figure identifies himself as Bertram and explains his punishment for sowing discord between father and son.
